libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Do not add a direct commons-compiler dependency just to fix the compile error: org.codehaus.janino:janino:3.1.0 already declares org.codehaus.janino:commons-compiler:3.1.0 transitively. The failing import org.codehaus.janino.util.iterator.Iterables was removed from the janino jar in 3.1.0; if an iterator utility test is still useful, use org.codehaus.commons.compiler.util.iterator.Iterables from the transitive commons-compiler jar, or avoid testing that transitive utility when focusing on janino coverage. Preserve/add the existing native-image build setup '--enable-url-protocols=jrt' for tests that exercise Janino compiler/evaluator APIs on JDK 9+ module runtimes. Tests that cook/evaluate/compile Java source should prove JVM behavior but may need to tolerate native-image runtime failures caused by unsupported dynamic class loading.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8855 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.codehaus.janino:janino:3.1.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Testing relocated commons-compiler utilities may shift coverage away from the target janino artifact.

Optional Ant integration exists but is not required for normal Janino usage or meaningful compiler/evaluator coverage; adding org.apache.ant:ant would only be justified if tests deliberately cover AntCompilerAdapter.
Native Image cannot generally load Janino-generated classes at runtime, so tests must distinguish expected native-image limitations from real regressions.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Janino needs no extra Maven dependency or Docker service; meaningful tests can use its in-memory compiler/evaluator APIs, but generated tests should account for the 3.1.0 utility-package relocation and existing native-image JRT protocol needs.
